input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS → iOS + Android.

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS builds)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- **Version discovery:** MAUI release notes list the minimum Xcode version. Check [github.com/dotnet/maui/releases](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) or `dotnet workload search` after installing .NET.
- **Post-install:**
  ```bash
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch    # installs required components
  xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version`

---

## 2. .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — grab the latest LTS or STS that supports your target MAUI version.
- **Version discovery:** The MAUI NuGet page ([nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Maui.Sdk](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Maui.Sdk)) lists the minimum .NET SDK. Currently .NET 8 (LTS) or .NET 9.
- **Install:** Download the macOS arm64/x64 installer `.pkg`.
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version`

---

## 3. MAUI Workload

```bash
dotnet workload install maui
```

This pulls in **maui-ios**, **maui-android**, and all transitive dependencies (Android SDK, build tools, emulator images via the Android workload).

- **Verify:**
  ```bash
  dotnet workload list          # should show "maui"
  dotnet new maui --list        # should show MAUI templates
  ```

---

## 4. Android SDK & Emulator

The `maui` workload auto-installs the Android SDK under `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx` (or the path shown by `dotnet android sdk info`). If you need additional API levels or emulator images:

```bash
dotnet android sdk install --package "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
dotnet android avd create --name Pixel_API34 --package "system-images;android-34;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
```

Alternatively, install **Android Studio** ([developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)) for a GUI SDK manager and emulator.

- **Verify:** `dotnet android sdk info`

---

## 5. JDK (Android requirement)

The MAUI Android workload bundles **Microsoft OpenJDK 17**. If it doesn't, or you need to manage it yourself:

- **Source:** [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)
- **Verify:** `java -version` (should show 17+)

---

## 6. (Optional) IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI ext** | [marketplace.visualstudio.com](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/) |
| **JetBrains Rider** (native MAUI support) | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

---

## 7. End-to-end verification

```bash
# Create, build, and run a test project
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# iOS (requires Xcode simulator)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios        # or net9.0-ios
dotnet run   -f net8.0-ios

# Android (requires emulator or device)
dotnet build -f net8.0-android
dotnet run   -f net8.0-android
```

A successful build with no errors confirms the full toolchain is working.

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → verify with a new project. The workload handles Android SDK + JDK automatically.
